title: Miley Cyrus Marks 20 Years Of Hannah Montana With New Single “Younger You”
author: Margaret Farrell
summary:

The post Miley Cyrus Marks 20 Years Of <em>Hannah Montana</em> With New Single “Younger You” appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Before the public knew her as Miley Cyrus, she was Hannah Montana — a fake pop star that then became a very big, real pop star. This week, Cyrus is celebrating her roots — the wig where it all began. She put together a TV special that featured both of her parents, an interview with Call Her Daddy hostess Alex Cooper, and even a special appearance from Chappell Roan. She also released a new single called "Younger You."

"Younger You" is a touching acoustic ballad about staying connected to a past version of yourself. It's easy to forget where we came from. Cyrus advocates for staying close to that inner child: "Somewhere along the way we lost touch/ We used to be so happy just because I know you had to go eventually/ But don't forget about me." 

Last night, Cyrus also accepted the Innovator award at iHeartRadio Music Awards. "My double denim and gold scarf thank you because they've been waiting over a decade to leave the house," She said in her acceptance speech. "Hannah Montana inspired me so much in my own career. I fell in love with the idea that you can create a persona that gives you the confidence and the courage to perform authentically. And that sometimes the fake you can actually reveal the real you."

Listen to to the single and check out her whole speech below.
